commit.message:
Fix gallery not showing all photos with same title

buildGallery used .find() which only returned the first before/after
photo per title+category group. Photos with the same title (e.g.
"Untitled") in the same category were being dropped. Now uses .filter()
to get all before/after photos, pairs them up, and shows any unpaired
ones as individual images.

feat: nixnet v1 engine — identity persistence proven on Proxmox

WHAT: Complete CLI skeleton for host identity and continuity framework.
Enrollment, convergent apply, layered config (global/role/host),
lifecycle state machine, doctor health checks, reclaim safety.

PROVEN: Fresh enroll, idempotent apply, dormant transition, identity
persistence after VM destruction, clean reclaim with claim history,
doctor 9/9 on both fresh and reclaimed machines. Two Proxmox VMs
(Ubuntu 24.04) tested and destroyed 2026-03-08.

ARCHITECTURE: Public engine (this repo) + private world (nixnet-world).
Engine locates world via NIXNET_WORLD env var. Local runtime at
~/.local/nixnet/. Pure bash, yq for YAML, no external frameworks.

TAGS: nixnet, identity, enrollment, lifecycle, reclaim, convergent-apply,
layered-config, proxmox, proof, milestone

commit.message:
feat: Helius holder quality & deep intelligence for OnChain Agent

Add Helius API integration for holder analysis with two layers:
- Holder Quality: top 10 concentration, whale count, distribution breakdown
- Holder Intelligence: sniper detection, wallet age analysis, connected
  wallet detection, dev wallet tracking

Uses DAS RPC (getTokenAccounts) for holder data and Enhanced Transactions
API for wallet history. Includes rate-limit handling with retry/backoff,
in-memory transaction cache, and batched concurrent requests.

Frontend displays Holder Quality and Holder Intelligence blocks in
On-Chain Metrics with risk badges, distribution bars, sniper details,
and wallet age breakdowns.

commit.message:
Rename bg_busy to poll_busy, add contention doc

Rename bg_busy → poll_busy for clarity — it guards poll cycles and list
fetches, not generic background work. Add docs/bg-busy-contention.md
documenting the busy flag contention issue and fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove broken webClientId from Google Sign-In; bump build 10

On iOS @react-native-google-signin reads the client ID directly from
GoogleService-Info.plist. Passing the old iOS client ID as webClientId
(serverClientID) causes Google's OAuth server to reject the flow because
it expects a web client here. With webClientId unset, iOS returns a valid
idToken for the plist client which Firebase Auth accepts.

Apple Sign-In fix requires a Firebase Console change (see PR description).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use absolute URLs in middleware redirects to prevent malformed URL errors

produce relative URLs, triggering "URL is malformed" errors. Now uses
explicit origin fallback to https://painclinics.com.

commit.message:
Fix container user: run as uid 1000 as required by HF Spaces

HF Spaces enforces uid 1000 for Docker containers. Without a USER
directive the container fails to start silently (hardware.current stays
null, CMD never executes). Also switches to full uvicorn path and sets
HOME to the actual user home.

commit.message:
Add hooks, /review command, and project settings

Hooks (auto-run by Claude Code):
- PostToolUse syntax_check.py: checks Python syntax after every file edit, exits 2 to alert Claude on error
- PreToolUse secrets_guard.py: blocks 'git add .' and 'git add -A' to prevent accidental secrets.toml exposure

Commands:
- /review: audits all pending changes, checks for secrets/bugs/debug code, gives go/no-go verdict

Both hooks tested and verified: syntax check catches real SyntaxErrors, secrets guard correctly returns exit code 2 to block unsafe commands.

commit.message:
feat: add min_action_interval to trust model — trigger-agnostic temporal spacing

Decouples temporal spacing guarantee from cron. The trust budget gates
total actions; min_action_interval gates action rate. Both must pass.
Default 300s (5 min) → 20 credits drain in ≥100 minutes regardless of
triggering mechanism (cron, post-receive, filesystem watch, manual).

- ef1-trust-model.md: new parameter, enforcement ordering, trigger table
- autonomous-sync.sh: check_interval() after budget check, migration-safe
- schema.sql: v9 — min_action_interval column on trust_budget

commit.message:
feat: per-stream tier engine, keyword space/hyphen normalization, crawl fixes

Per-stream tier engine (Phase 2): each category/endpoint is an independent
stream with its own tier structure. API sites get 1 stream with modification-
date filtering; HTML sites get N streams with page-range division across tiers.
Includes staleness-based rotation and firearms domain priority plugin (3x/2x).

Keyword matching: spaces, hyphens, and no separator now treated as equivalent
in model names. "AR-15", "AR 15", "AR15" all match each other via flexible
regex. SQL queries include stripped variants for DB pre-filtering.

Crawl fixes: pagination resume bug (saves currentPageUrl on token exhaustion),
WooCommerce modified_after/modified_before for restock detection, Shopify
updated_at_min, Playwright fallback on empty HTML.

Per-site crawl tuning: admin-adjustable token budget, tier cooldowns, and
catalog share percentages via crawlTuning JSON column.

Absorb gherkin compat modules: 23 new stdlib libraries

Adapted from gherkin/src/compat/ with Gerbil-compatible APIs on
native Chez Scheme. No Gambit dependencies.

New modules:
- std/pregexp: full regex engine (Dorai Sitaram's pregexp, 723 lines)
- std/test: test framework (test-suite, test-case, check)
- std/logger: logging with levels (error/warn/info/debug/verbose)
- std/cli/getopt: CLI argument parsing (options, flags, commands)
- std/misc/thread: Gambit-compatible thread API on Chez threads
- std/misc/process: run-process, run-process/batch
- std/misc/queue: mutable FIFO queue
- std/misc/bytes: bytevector bitwise operations
- std/misc/uuid: UUID v4 generation
- std/misc/repr: repr/prn object printing
- std/misc/completion: async completion tokens
- std/text/base64: base64 encode/decode
- std/text/hex: hex encode/decode
- std/text/utf8: UTF-8 utilities
- std/text/csv: CSV read/write
- std/text/xml: SXML → XML serialization
- std/os/env: getenv/setenv/unsetenv
- std/os/temporaries: temp file creation
- std/os/signal: POSIX signal constants + handlers
- std/os/fdio: file descriptor I/O
- std/srfi/srfi-13: SRFI-13 string operations
- std/srfi/srfi-19: date/time handling
- std/crypto/digest: MD5/SHA via openssl CLI

All 289 tests pass (213 existing + 76 new).

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement get_page_text tool (Spec 009) (#6)

* feat: implement get_page_text tool (Spec 009)

Adds get_page_text MCP tool that extracts human-readable text from the
active tab. Uses a priority chain (article → main → [role=main] → body),
operates on a DOM clone to avoid mutating the live page, strips noise
elements (script/style/noscript/aria-hidden; nav/header/footer in body
fallback), and truncates at 100 000 chars.

- ClaudeInSafari Extension/Resources/tools/get-page-text.js
- Tests/JS/get-page-text.test.js (15 tests, all passing)
- manifest.json and background.js load-order comment updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update ROADMAP and README to reflect Phase 4 completion

- Mark all Phase 4 tools (read_page, find, form_input, get_page_text) ✅
- Mark Phase 4 phase header ✅
- Mark H4 (tool-registry tests) as resolved in First Build checklist
- README: fix wire protocol description (newline-delimited JSON, not 4-byte prefix)
- README: mark form_input, get_page_text, tabs_context/create as Done

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review findings for get_page_text (Spec 009)

Critical:
- Wrap innerText access in try/finally so DOM container is always
  removed from document.body even when innerText throws

Important:
- Guard document.body null before appendChild (teardown/empty pages)
- Catch and reclassify resolveTab errors with tabs_context_mcp guidance,
  consistent with read-page.js
- Use result.__error != null instead of truthiness (catches empty string)
- Add typeof result.text !== 'string' guard to prevent undefined
  propagating as MCP protocol violation
- Normalize articles[0] || null to avoid undefined root
- Expand @throws JSDoc to document all five distinct throw paths
- Fix JSDoc to accurately describe the brief DOM mutation (clone is
  attached to body in off-screen container, then removed)
- Document the articles.length === 1 tie-breaking rule in step comment

Tests (T16-T20):
- resolveTab failure surfaces tabs_context_mcp guidance
- [null] result rejects with no-result error
- Non-string result.text rejects with shape error
- Empty-string __error still rejects (was silently bypassed)
- Injected code contains try/finally + removeChild

STRUCTURE.md: add get-page-text.test.js to canonical layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Agent 会话重命名时附加目录丢失 (#96)

修复 Agent 模式下重命名会话时，附加的文件夹（attachedDirectories）会丢失的问题。

问题原因：
- 前端在处理重命名时，手动构造了只包含 title 和 updatedAt 的对象
- 导致后端返回的完整 AgentSessionMeta 对象中的其他字段（如 attachedDirectories）被丢弃

修复方案：
- 使用后端返回的完整 AgentSessionMeta 对象更新状态
- 与 Chat 模式的重命名逻辑保持一致

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
P42: Think Like a Fabricator — finish pipeline fix, Opus BOM, aluminum weights, subtotal refresh

- Finish is now always required: is_complete/get_completion_status treat finish as mandatory even when not in tree's required_fields. Finish question injected at session start when not extracted from description. Fixes LED sign silently defaulting to "raw" ($0 finish).
- Opus BOM estimation: new opus_estimate_bom() in hardware_sourcer.py sends description + cut list to AI for hardware + consumables. Wired in pricing_engine.py (extends hardware, replaces consumables). Falls back gracefully when unavailable.
- Aluminum weights: 10 al_ entries in STOCK_WEIGHTS + density-ratio fallback (steel * 0.344) in _aggregate_materials for unknown aluminum profiles.
- Subtotal refresh: material/hardware/consumable subtotal elements now have IDs, _recalcTotals() updates all 6 section subtotals.
- Hardware install calibration: structured block for structural (0.5-2 hrs), electronics (4-6 hrs min), gate operators (2-3 hrs).
- Knowledge vs preference distinction added to suggest_additional_questions prompt.
- 11 new tests, 904 total passing.
